o: Forged by Sauron in Orodruin, c. S.A. 1600, to rule the others
d: 'One Ring to rule them all'. Tolkien nowhere quantifies how much of Sauron's power passed into it; the Ring holds “a great part of his former power” [C], and the often-quoted fraction is not his [I].
ch: App B; Silm, Of the Rings
f: Unmade where it was made; with it fell the Dark Tower.

n: The Three Rings — Narya, Nenya, Vilya
o: Celebrimbor alone, Eregion, c. S.A. 1590 — never touched by Sauron
d: Fire, adamant and sapphire; preservation and kindling, not dominion.
ch: Celebrimbor — sent them away before the sack
Gil-galad took Vilya and Narya; Galadriel Nenya
Vilya to Elrond; Narya to Círdan
Círdan gave Narya to Gandalf at his landing
borne over Sea by the Three Keepers
f: Their power ended with the One; they went West with their keepers.
x: UT notes divergent accounts of when Gil-galad passed Narya — 'at first' vs at the Last Alliance; both recorded.

o: Eregion's smiths with Sauron's aid, c. S.A. 1500-1590
d: Given to Dwarf-kings and Men; the Seven bred gold and wrath, the Nine made wraiths.
ch: Sauron seized them at Eregion's fall; distributed
the Seven: four consumed by dragons, three regathered
the last of the Seven taken from Thráin II in Dol Guldur
f: All accounted to Sauron or the fire before the end.

o: Wrought by Fëanor in Valinor, Y.T. 1449-50; hallowed by Varda — 'the fire of the Trees' locked in crystal
d: The three great jewels over which every war of the First Age was fought; 'the Doom of the Noldor' bound within them.
ch: one cut free by Beren and Lúthien
Eärendil — bound on his brow, raised as the Star
the last two: Maedhros to the fiery chasm, Maglor to the sea
f: One in the heavens, one in the earth, one in the sea — 'and so the Silmarils found their long homes'.

o: Found in the deeps of Erebor by Thráin I, T.A. 1999
d: The Heart of the Mountain — “The great jewel shone before his feet of its own inner light”.
ch: laid on Thorin's breast in the deep tomb
f: Under the Mountain forever, with Orcrist above it.

o: The Necklace of the Dwarves, made of old for Finrod Felagund
d: Fairest work of Nogrod and Belegost; deadliest gift of the First Age.
ch: Húrin bore it from the sacked halls to Thingol — 'thy fee'
HoME XI, Tale of Years — which gives two schemes (501/502 and 502/503) [conflict]
remade about a Silmaril; Thingol slain over it by the smiths of Nogrod
HoME XI, Tale of Years — which gives two schemes (501/502 and 502/503) [conflict]
recovered at Sarn Athrad; Lúthien wore it at Tol Galen
Dior, then Elwing — into the Sea and to Eärendil
f: Its Silmaril rides the sky; of the necklace no more is said.

o: Two traditions: made in Gondolin by Enerdhil the jewel-smith — or by Celebrimbor in Eregion
d: The green stone of healing whose light makes all things fair; it named a king.
ch: (tradition one) Enerdhil → Idril → Eärendil, into the sky
(tradition two, or a second stone) Celebrimbor → Galadriel
Galadriel → Celebrían → Arwen? — 'by right of kin'; given to Aragorn at Lórien
Aragorn Elessar — crowned in its name
f: On the King's breast; UT prints both makings side by side.
x: UT's two accounts are irreconcilable and both kept — the archive's rule made visible.

o: Telchar of Nogrod, First Age
d: The sword that cut the Ring from Sauron's hand — broken, kept, reforged as the Flame of the West.
ch: Telchar's forge, Nogrod — 'Telchar first wrought it in the deeps of time'
heirloom to Elendil (the road between unstated — honest gap)
broken beneath Elendil at the Siege
the Shards, heirloom of Isildur's line
given to Aragorn with the ring of Barahir
f: Hangs at the King's side in the Fourth Age.

o: Gondolin-work of the First Age — a long knife of the Eldar, a sword for a hobbit
d: Glows blue at orcs; named by its owner in Mirkwood.
ch: Frodo; borne by Sam through Cirith Ungol
with Sam, then his line? — the corpus does not say beyond
f: Last seen at the Ring-bearer's side.

o: Turgon's own blade and its mate, Gondolin, First Age
d: Foe-hammer and Goblin-cleaver — the trolls' hoard gave back the Hidden King's sword.
ch: Turgon; the armouries of the Hidden City
plunder-road unstated: 'they came from Gondolin' — six millennia dark
Glamdring: Gandalf, through Moria and the war; Orcrist: on Thorin's tomb
f: One sailed West at a wizard's side; one warns the Mountain of foes.

o: Forged by Eöl of meteoric iron ('iron that fell from heaven as a blazing star')
d: The black sword with a will of its own; it spoke once, at the end.
ch: Eöl of Nan Elmoth — given to Thingol as fee for Nan Elmoth
Beleg Strongbow — against the counsel of Melian
Túrin — who slew Beleg with it; reforged black: Gurthang
broken beneath Túrin at Cabed Naeramarth; buried with him
f: 'I will drink thy blood gladly' — its last words; shards under the standing stone.

o: Telchar of Nogrod; the image of Glaurung on its crest
d: Its wearer feared no stroke — and was known to every eye.
ch: UT, Narn (the passage CT removed from CoH); date c.416 from HoME XI
Fingon to Hador with the lordship of Dor-lómin
UT, Narn (the passage CT removed from CoH); date c.416 from HoME XI
Hador → Galdor → Húrin (who wore it not) → Túrin
UT, Narn (the passage CT removed from CoH); date c.416 from HoME XI
borne by Túrin in Dor-Cúarthol — 'the Helm of Hador is come again'
UT, Narn (the passage CT removed from CoH); date c.416 from HoME XI
f: Passes out of the tale with Nargothrond's sack; the corpus follows it no further.

o: Wrought for an elf-prince of old; given to Bilbo from Smaug's hoard
d: 'Worth more than the whole Shire and everything in it' — Gandalf.
ch: lent to the Mathom-house of Michel Delving; recalled, and given to Frodo
saved Frodo from the Moria-spear; taken by the Tower; restored at Cormallen
f: With Frodo over Sea — or left? the corpus does not say; the gap stands. Last attested wearing: the Scouring of the Shire, 3 Nov 3019 [C].

o: The Hammer of the Underworld — Morgoth's mace; its name given again to the wolf-headed ram
d: The first broke the ground before Fingolfin; the second broke the Gate of Gondor.
ch: the ram of Minas Morgul, 'a hundred feet in length', hauled by great beasts
f: The Gate it broke was rebuilt in mithril and steel by Gimli's folk.

o: 'It is said that they were made by Fëanor' in Aman
d: The Seven Seeing-stones of the Realms in Exile; the master-stone abode in Avallónë.
ch: gift of the Eldar to Amandil's house
UT, The Palantíri; Silm, Of the Rings
seven placed: Annúminas, Amon Sûl, Elostirion; Osgiliath, Minas Ithil, Minas Anor, Orthanc
Amon Sûl & Annúminas lost with Arvedui in the Ice-bay
Anor-stone: Denethor's despair; Orthanc-stone: to the King
f: Two under the Ice-bay, one in the River, one burned hands in the White Tower, one serves the King, one looks on Eressëa no more.

o: The light of Eärendil's star caught in Galadriel's fountain-water
d: 'A light to you in dark places, when all other lights go out.'
ch: against Shelob in the pass; against the Watchers of the Tower
borne over Sea in the Ring-bearer's hand
f: Went West still shining.

o: Finrod's ring of Valinor — twin serpents with eyes of emerald, crowned with flowers of gold
d: The oldest heirloom of the line of kings — a Elf-lord's oath made metal.
ch: given to Barahir for the saving of his life at the Bragollach
recovered from the Orcs by Beren, with his father's hand
Beren → Dior → the line of Elros in Númenor
Silmariën's line: the Lords of Andúnië
Elendil → the kings of Arnor → Arvedui
pledged to the Lossoth of Forochel; ransomed back
the Chieftains → Aragorn → given to Arwen on Cerin Amroth
f: On Arwen's hand; the one object that passes through the whole legendarium.

o: Telperion the Eldest of Trees — its image Galathilion, made by Yavanna for the Elves of Tirion
d: A living heirloom: light handed down as seed and sapling through three ages.
ch: Telperion of Valinor — slain by Melkor & Ungoliant
Galathilion in Tirion → Celeborn of Tol Eressëa
Nimloth of Númenor — Isildur steals the fruit; the Tree burned by Sauron
S.A. between 3262 and 3319 [I-M — the burning is undated]
the First White Tree of Minas Ithil — burned by Sauron
second and third Trees of Minas Anor; the Withered Tree
the sapling found on Mindolluin by the King and Mithrandir
f: Flowering in the Court of the Fountain, Fourth Age.

o: Out of the hoard of Scatha the Worm, brought south by Eorl's folk
d: Small, silver-wound, of dwarf-make: he that blows it “He that blows it at need shall set fear in the hearts of his enemies and joy in the hearts of his friends”.
ch: the hoard of Scatha; the Éothéod
Éowyn's gift to Meriadoc at the parting
blown at the raising of the Shire — Bywater
f: An heirloom of Buckland thereafter (the Yule-horns of the Shire).

o: The war-summons of Gondor to the Mark
d: One black-feathered arrow, its head painted red — six days from errand to answer.
ch: Hirgon of Gondor to Théoden at Dunharrow
Hirgon slain riding back; the arrow found by the fallen
the beacons: Amon Dîn to Halifirien, burning westward
f: Answered: six thousand spears at dawn.
